title: Delegate all writes to CLI; extension remains a pure JSON reader
context: As the extension grew from a read-only tree viewer into a full in-editor surface with write-back capability (Record Invariant, Record Bug), the question arose: should the extension write directly to .hunch/ JSON, or should it defer to the CLI?
decision: The extension remains a pure reader of committed JSON. All writes (Record Invariant, Record Bug) delegate to the `hunch` CLI via atomic paths. The extension never edits .hunch/ JSON itself.
consequences: Users must have the CLI installed and on PATH to write records; extension is write-capable only when paired with CLI
Data corruption impossible; all validation and atomic writes centralized in the CLI, no concurrent write races
Single source of truth for validation logic; no duplication between extension and CLI
Extension stays lightweight (no native deps, no filesystem perms beyond reading)
CLI remains the canonical authority over the .hunch/ schema and invariants
alternatives_rejected: Direct JSON write capability in TypeScript — risks concurrent writes, schema corruption, duplicated validation logic
Lock-file or filesystem mutex in extension — complex, error-prone, platform-specific
Embed validation logic in extension — couples CLI and extension versioning, breaks single-schema principle
